Issues: The current image is highly stylized and vibrant, which is attractive, but it lacks professional polish necessary for high-value commercial sales. The aesthetic is very broad and could be easily replicated.
The color saturation is extremely high. While this is a stylistic choice, overly saturated images can sometimes look cheap or amateur when scaled or printed, reducing perceived quality.

The technical execution (line quality, shading, and composition) is very simple. To increase commercial appeal, more detailed texture, refined gradients, or a unique artistic signature would be necessary.
As an intellectual property consideration, if the artist is selling this, ensuring the terms of use and licensing are clear is crucial to protect their rights.
